title: IaP Provider Mapping v1
description: Schema for provider mapping artifacts (*.iap-map.yaml). A mapping is a pure function from a canonical IaP document to a provider plan: byte-identical canonical output for identical inputs, no ambient lookups at mapping time. Mappings are fail-closed: a document using any kind, field, or value outside the declared support matrix MUST be rejected. Mappings never modify IaP semantics.

provider.description: Provider namespace this mapping realizes (matches the extension namespace grammar).

specCompat.description: IaP specification version range this mapping supports (semver range expression).

mappings.description: One entry per supported IaP kind.

fieldPath.description: Dot path into the resource entry (e.g. spec.availability).

supports.description: Fail-closed coverage matrix. fields lists every supported field path; values constrains supported enum values per field. Anything outside this matrix MUST cause rejection, never silent dropping.

values.description: Per-field allowed values, keyed by field path.

relationships.description: Relationship verbs this mapping can realize for edges whose source is this kind.

realize.description: Ordered realization rules. The first rule whose when-clause matches applies; rules are evaluated deterministically.

when.description: Field-path -> exact-value match conditions; all must hold. Omit to always apply.

targets.description: Provider resource types produced (provider-namespaced identifiers, e.g. aws:rds:DBInstance).

derive.description: Deterministic derivations of provider attributes from IaP fields: each entry maps a provider attribute to a source field and a value map or constant.

map.description: Exact IaP value -> provider value lookup. MUST cover every supported value of the source field.

outputs.description: Bindings of the kind's abstract output attributes (endpoint, connectionSecret, identifier, ...) to provider plan attributes. Every abstract attribute the core spec declares for this kind MUST be bound.
